subject: SIP weekly update - week ending 6 Feb

content.answer:
Hi Ian and all,

Please find attached for review - 1) SIP weekly update; 2) Swarm weekly
update.

Thank you.
Cuiwei

content.question:
Thanks.



*SIP*



Slide 8

   - For ID/VN export, not much increase in seller MOM?

Slide 21

   - Local SG sellers have high ABS but are PC2 positive?

Not sure the
   reason make sense?
- In fact the higher the ABS, the higher chance PC2 should be positive?

Others

   - I am not sure direct is growing fast enough



*Swarm*



Slide 2

   - Why drop so much MOM?

Slide 3

   - The table is hard to understand in general. What is target for Feb,
   and what is actual for each channel?
- For 炒手, how many sellers do we expect from them in Feb?

Other

   - When will we get the breakdown of UE for incubation/mature SKUs?

content.answer:
Hi Ian，

Please find our responses *in blue* below, also included the follow-up
question from last weekly on ID/VN direct target *in red*.

Slide 8

   -

   For ID/VN export, not much increase in seller MOM? Drop in SKUs MOM? How
   Sai Kit <saikit.how@shopee.com> Emily Pang Jee Suan
   <emily.pangjs@shopee.com>
   -

      Seller MoM growth slowdown is expected and reflected in FY26 plan.
      -

         We completed R1 mass pitching and have shifted to 1:1 repitching
         of higher-quality Mall/managed sellers, which reduces volume
but improves
         seller quality.
         -

      *Temporary capacity factors also impacted active sellers coun*t, but
      they are seasonal and won’t change FY26 growth trajectory
      -

         The ID team prioritized Raya push and paused mass pitching.
         -

         Some VN sellers went on Tet holiday and shifted shops to HM.
         -

      SKU count drop in ID and VN is not expected and mainly driven by 2
      reasons
      -

         VN sellers shifted shops to HM (~100k SKU drop)
         -

         VN/ID SKU cleanup by import markets based on import market listing
         limit policy (~400k SKU drop) - this is a known BAU regional
ops cleanup;
         didn’t surface earlier as the fast growth of SKUs in the
previous months
         shielded this impact. We will talk to regional ops post CNY
to explore a
         longer grace period (currently 2 months) before cleanup.



Slide 21 Brenda Lim Ler Shyuan <brenda.limls@shopee.com>

   -

   Local SG sellers have high ABS but are PC2 positive? Not sure the reason
   make sense?
   -

   In fact the higher the ABS, the higher chance PC2 should be positive?


Table: MY import: MP PC2 UE comparison across BUs


[image: image.png]

   -

   You are right, the key issue is revenue generated lower than PRM cost,
   despite high ABS.
   -

   From the benchmark table above for MY import

+ @Ivy Chen <ivy.chen01@shopee.com> @Leo Chen <leo.chenyh@shopee.com> for
Swarm acquisition.

Hi Ian,

Please see our *responses below in green* (questions in red).

Slide 21 Brenda Lim Ler Shyuan <brenda.limls@shopee.com>

   -

   Local SG sellers have high ABS but are PC2 positive? Not sure the reason
   make sense?
   -

   In fact the higher the ABS, the higher chance PC2 should be positive?


[image: image.png]

   -

   You are right, the key issue is revenue generated lower than PRM cost,
   despite high ABS.
   -

   From the benchmark table above for MY import, Direct revenue is 22% ABS
   while PRM is 23% ABS; as a benchmark, MY local orders revenue is 21% of
   ABS while PRM is 20%.
   -

   The gap on revenue is due lower paid ads revenue: this is a known issue
   as ads adoption/spending is still low due to 1) top-up challenge without
   global wallet; 2) ads ROI worse than their local shops.
   -

   By Apr-26, revenue will improve with global wallet launch + incentive
   packages to fix paid ads gap.


How about SLS revenues, where do they sit? Chiong Zhi Ann
<zhiann.chiong@shopee.com>


Table: MY import program UE (MP + SLS)


[image: image.png]



   -

   Added SLS P&L and total program P&L view (MP + SLS) for MY market. SLS
   P&L is booked in the import market.
   -

   Direct SLS profit per order is $0.5, on par with CNCB. Margin is
   achieved with higher average parcel weight (0.6kg Direct vs 0.26kg CNCB).
   -

   Including SLS, total program P&L for mature Direct sellers at +$0.1 per
   order.


We do charge ads growth program for CN CB sellers, do we see ourselves
charging it for direct sellers? How Sai Kit <saikit.how@shopee.com> Yisi
Chen <yisi.chen@shopee.com>


Table: summary ads growth program plan in local markets and CB BUs

[image: image.png]



   -

   Current
   -

      All Direct sellers will be enrolled in the optional ads growth
      program f

content.question: what do we need to push ID direct to grow faster?
